content.description:
The Element Inspector is a runtime debugging tool that lets you identify which item and view is responsible for any rendered element on screen.

## Activation

Add `?debug` to the URL:

```
https://hobson.app/?debug#item-uuid
```

Or activate via the REPL:

```javascript
window.hobsonDebug = true;
```

## Crosshair Mode

When active, moving the mouse highlights DOM elements with colored overlays. Each overlay shows:

- **Item ID** — which item this element belongs to
- **View name** — which view rendered it
- **Render instance** — the specific render instance in the instance registry

## How It Works

1. The rendering system sets `data-item-id` and `data-view-id` attributes on rendered elements
2. The inspector listens for `mousemove` events
3. On hover, it walks up the DOM tree to find the nearest `[data-item-id]` ancestor
4. Displays an overlay with the item/view information
5. Clicking navigates to the item in the REPL

## Use Cases

- **Debugging rendering issues** — "which view is rendering this wrong?"
- **Understanding the render tree** — "what's the nesting structure here?"
- **Finding item IDs** — quickly get the ID of any visible item
- **View identification** — which view code to edit for a given element

## See Also

- [kernel:rendering-system](item://33333333-5555-0000-0000-000000000000) — Sets data attributes during render
- [REPL](item://c0c0c0c0-0008-0000-0000-000000000000) — Alternative debugging via the console
